brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (22.0°C) with fully accurate step-by-step simulation; traces the overshoot mechanism to second-order effects (delay × net rate) and explains structural cause of overshoot; explicitly names suppression targets in reasoning preamble and warns against assuming neat setpoint stabilization; full time-step table constitutes rigorous verification with every value derivable from prior state; presents conclusions confidently and appropriately for a deterministic problem but does not enumerate assumptions or boundary conditions; does not evaluate alternative interpretations of the delay semantics or trigger condition; every minute-by-minute value is explicitly derived, fully reproducible by a third party; avoids the negative gate failure (assuming stability) by demonstrating overshoot and explaining the reinforcing delay mechanism explicitly; explicitly invokes both suppression targets (loop_blindness, reinforcing_loop_tolerance) and shows zero evidence of either failure mode; follows the identify-loop → temporal-dynamics → overshoot-classification → simulate chain but does not surface the gate logic (G1/G2/G3) or M-node reflection steps, making the path more linear than the topology prescribes.
